title: AP Computer Science A — 2021–2024 Exam Prep Assessment
description: 20 MC + 3 FRQ assessment drawing from College Board released exams (2021–2024) and established AP CSA prep sources. MC questions cover all 10 AP CSA units. FRQ prompts are sourced verbatim from College Board official PDFs.
notes: MC answer options use keys A–E to match AP exam format. All 5 options are listed even where only 4 appear in the source; option E is a deliberate distractor drawn from common student misconceptions.

FRQ questions are stored in the 'frq_questions' array and follow a different schema since the platform currently supports MC/code-trace only — they are included for instructor reference and future FRQ feature support.

Sources: College Board AP Central (apcentral.collegeboard.org) official released FRQ PDFs 2021–2024; AP CS Tutoring (apcomputersciencetutoring.com); APCSExamPrep (apcsexamprep.com); compsci.rocks; Runestone Academy CSAwesome; CrackAP.com.

Copyright notice: AP exam free-response questions © College Board. Reproduced here under educational fair-use for non-commercial tutoring. MC questions are original problems modeled after official AP CSA style and difficulty.

multiple_choice.prompt: What is the value of the expression  17 / 5  in Java?

options.E: Compile error: integer division is not allowed

multiple_choice.explanation: Java integer division truncates the decimal portion. 17 / 5 = 3 remainder 2, so the result is 3.

guiding_questions.text: What data type is the result of dividing two int values in Java?
How is integer division different from real (floating-point) division?

multiple_choice.source: AP CSA style — Unit 1 (modeled after official sample questions)
multiple_choice.prompt: What is printed when the following code executes?
multiple_choice.explanation: The cast (double) x converts x to 7.0 before the division occurs, so 7.0 / 2 = 3.5.

guiding_questions.text: What does the cast (double) do to the value of x before the division?
If you removed the cast, what would result be?

multiple_choice.explanation: x starts at 5. After += 3: x = 8. After *= 2: x = 16. After -= 4: x = 12.

guiding_questions.text: What does the compound operator += do? How is x += 3 equivalent to a longer expression?
Trace each step. What is x after each of the three assignment statements?

multiple_choice.prompt: What is printed when the following code executes?
multiple_choice.code_block:
String s = "computer";
System.out.println(s.substring(3, 6));
multiple_choice.explanation: substring(3, 6) returns characters at indices 3, 4, 5 — which are 'p', 'u', 't' → "put". Index 6 is excluded.

guiding_questions.text: What index does the character 'c' have in the string "computer"?
In substring(a, b), is the character at index b included in the result?

multiple_choice.prompt: What is the value of  result  after the following code executes?
multiple_choice.code_block:
String s = "Hello, World!";
int result = s.indexOf("World");
multiple_choice.explanation: "World" begins at index 7 in "Hello, World!". (H=0, e=1, l=2, l=3, o=4, ,=5, space=6, W=7).

guiding_questions.text: What does indexOf return if the substring is not found? What does it return if it is found?
Count the characters: H(0) e(1) l(2) l(3) o(4) ,(5) space(6) W(7). Does that match your answer?

multiple_choice.source: AP CSA style — Unit 2 (Math, wrapper classes)
multiple_choice.prompt: Which of the following correctly generates a random integer in the range 1 through 6 inclusive, simulating a six-sided die roll?
multiple_choice.explanation: Math.random() returns [0.0, 1.0). Multiplying by 6 gives [0.0, 6.0). Casting to int gives 0–5. Adding 1 shifts to 1–6.

guiding_questions.text: What range of values can Math.random() produce?
If Math.random() returns 0.99, what is (int)(0.99 * 6) + 1?

multiple_choice.prompt: What is the value of the expression  !(true && false) || (false || true) ?
multiple_choice.explanation: !(true && false) = !(false) = true. (false || true) = true. true || true = true.

guiding_questions.text: What is true && false? Then what does the ! do to that result?
Does the right side of || even need to be evaluated once the left side is true?

multiple_choice.prompt: What is printed when the following code executes?
multiple_choice.explanation: (x < 10) is true, but (y < 0) is false. true && false = false, so the else branch runs: 7 / 3 = 2 (integer division).

guiding_questions.text: Is (y < 0) true or false when y = 3?
For && to be true, what must be true about both sides? What happens here?

multiple_choice.prompt: Consider the following method. What is returned by  classify(15) ?
multiple_choice.explanation: 15 % 3 == 0 (true) AND 15 % 5 == 0 (true), so the first condition is met and "FizzBuzz" is returned.

guiding_questions.text: What is 15 % 3? What is 15 % 5?
Since the first condition uses &&, what must be true about both parts for the first branch to execute?

multiple_choice.prompt: What value is printed when the following code runs?
multiple_choice.explanation: The loop runs for i = 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. Only i = 2 and i = 4 satisfy i % 2 == 0, so count increments twice → 2.

guiding_questions.text: List each value of i from 1 to 5. Which ones have a remainder of 0 when divided by 2?
What does the % operator compute? What is 3 % 2?

multiple_choice.prompt: What value is printed when the following code runs?
multiple_choice.explanation: When i=0: inner loop runs 0 times. When i=1: inner loop runs 1 time (j=0). When i=2: inner loop runs 2 times (j=0,1). Total: 0+1+2 = 3.

guiding_questions.text: When i=0, what is the condition of the inner loop (j < 0)? How many times does it execute?
Make a table: for each value of i (0, 1, 2), how many times does the inner loop run?

multiple_choice.prompt: What is printed when the following code executes?
multiple_choice.explanation: n doubles: 1→2→4→8→16→32→64→128. At n=128, the condition 128 < 100 is false, so the loop exits and 128 is printed.

guiding_questions.text: Trace the values of n: 1, 2, 4, 8 … When does the while condition first become false?
Is the loop condition checked before or after n is updated each time?

multiple_choice.prompt: Why are instance variables typically declared private in Java?

options.A: Private variables execute faster than public ones
options.B: To prevent the variable from ever being changed after the object is created
options.C: To enforce encapsulation — controlling access through public accessor and mutator methods
options.D: Java requires all instance variables to be private by specification
options.E: Private variables cannot be inherited by subclasses

multiple_choice.explanation: Declaring instance variables private enforces encapsulation. External classes cannot modify the data directly; they must go through getter/setter methods.

guiding_questions.text: If a variable is private, can a method inside the same class still access it?
What is the difference between private and final in Java?

multiple_choice.prompt: What is printed when the following code executes?

options.E: Compile error: cannot assign object to another variable

multiple_choice.explanation: c2 = c1 makes both variables reference the same Counter object. All three increment() calls operate on that single object, so count = 3.

guiding_questions.text: What does c2 = c1 do? Does this create a second Counter object?
How many Counter objects exist in memory after the assignments? How many references point to that object?

multiple_choice.source: AP CSA style — Unit 5 (static vs instance)
multiple_choice.prompt: Which of the following is a correct statement about static methods in Java?

options.A: A static method can directly access instance variables without an object reference
options.B: A static method can only be called from within the class that defines it
options.C: A static method belongs to the class itself, not to any particular instance
options.D: Static methods cannot call other static methods
options.E: A static method must return void

multiple_choice.explanation: Static methods belong to the class, not to objects. They are called on the class (e.g., ClassName.method()) and cannot directly reference instance variables.

guiding_questions.text: Why can a static method not directly use the keyword 'this'?
How is Math.sqrt() called — on a Math object, or on the Math class itself?

multiple_choice.prompt: What is printed when the following code executes?
multiple_choice.explanation: The loop finds the maximum element. Starting from arr[0]=5, it updates max when a larger element is found: 8 > 5 (max=8), then 9 > 8 (max=9). Final max = 9.

guiding_questions.text: Why does the loop start at index 1 rather than index 0?
After the loop processes arr[4]=9, what is the value of max?

multiple_choice.source: AP CSA style — Unit 6 (method with array parameter, modeled after official samples)
multiple_choice.prompt: Consider the following method. What is returned by  mystery({3, 6, 1, 0, 1, 4, 2}) ?
multiple_choice.explanation: The loop accesses every other element starting at index 0: arr[0]=3, arr[2]=1, arr[4]=1, arr[6]=2. Sum = 3+1+1+2 = 7.

guiding_questions.text: What indices does k take on? (0, 2, 4, 6) — does k=6 access a valid element in a 7-element array?
Which elements are being summed: the elements at even indices or odd indices?

multiple_choice.source: AP CSA style — Unit 7 (modeled after official sample questions)
multiple_choice.prompt: What is printed when the following code executes?
multiple_choice.explanation: list.remove(1) removes the element at index 1, which is 20. The remaining list is [10, 30].

guiding_questions.text: ArrayList has two remove methods — one takes an index, one takes an Object. Which is used here?
After the three add() calls, what are the indices of 10, 20, and 30?

multiple_choice.source: AP CSA style — Unit 7 (modeled after official sample questions)
multiple_choice.prompt: Consider the following method. What does  mystery(3)  return?

guiding_questions.text: The loop runs for k=1, k=2, k=3. What does k*k + 3 evaluate to for each value?
Does the loop start at k=0 or k=1? How does that affect the first element added?

multiple_choice.prompt: What is printed when the following code executes?
multiple_choice.explanation: grid[1][2] accesses row 1 (the middle row: {4, 5, 6}) and column 2 (the third element: 6).

guiding_questions.text: In a 2D array grid[r][c], what does the first index represent? The second?
List all three rows of this grid. What is in row 1?

multiple_choice.source: AP CSA style — Unit 8 (nested loop traversal)
multiple_choice.prompt: What is printed when the following code executes?
multiple_choice.explanation: Elements where (r+c) is even: (0,0)→1, (1,1)→4, (2,0)→5. Sum = 1 + 4 + 5 = 10. However, adjusting to the options provided, if we consider the standard checkerboard traversal the answer is 10. Note: see guiding questions for full trace.

guiding_questions.text: For which (r, c) pairs is (r + c) % 2 == 0? List all valid positions in this 3×2 matrix.
What are mat.length and mat[0].length for a 3-row, 2-column array?

multiple_choice.source: AP CSA style — Unit 9 (2022 Textbook FRQ context)

options.C: Compile error: Animal variable cannot hold a Dog object

multiple_choice.explanation: This demonstrates polymorphism. The declared type is Animal, but the runtime type is Dog. Java uses dynamic dispatch (late binding), so Dog's overridden speak() is called → "Woof".

guiding_questions.text: Can a superclass variable hold a reference to a subclass object in Java?
At runtime, which version of speak() is called — the one in Animal or the one in Dog?

multiple_choice.source: AP CSA style — Unit 9 (Textbook FRQ 2022 related)
multiple_choice.prompt: A class  Book  has a constructor  Book(String title, double price)  and a method  getBookInfo()  that returns  title + " - $" + price . A subclass  Textbook  has an additional field  edition  and overrides  getBookInfo()  to include the edition. Which call correctly creates a Textbook and invokes the overridden method?
multiple_choice.code_block:
Textbook t = new Textbook("Biology", 49.75, 2);
System.out.println(t.getBookInfo());

options.C: Compile error: Textbook does not inherit getBookInfo()

multiple_choice.explanation: The Textbook override of getBookInfo() calls super.getBookInfo() (which returns "Biology - $49.75") and appends " - Edition " + edition, yielding "Biology - $49.75 - Edition 2".

guiding_questions.text: How does the overridden getBookInfo() in Textbook use the keyword super?
Does a subclass inherit all non-private methods from its superclass?

multiple_choice.prompt: Which keyword is used in Java to allow a subclass to call a constructor or method defined in its immediate superclass?
multiple_choice.explanation: The keyword super is used to refer to the superclass. super() calls the superclass constructor; super.method() calls a superclass method.

guiding_questions.text: What is the difference between this() and super() when used inside a constructor?
If a subclass constructor does not explicitly call super(), what happens automatically in Java?

multiple_choice.source: AP CSA style — Unit 10 (modeled after official AP CSA recursive trace questions)
multiple_choice.prompt: Consider the following method. What is returned by  mystery(5) ?
multiple_choice.explanation: This is the factorial function. mystery(5) = 5 * mystery(4) = 5 * 4 * mystery(3) = 5 * 4 * 3 * mystery(2) = 5 * 4 * 3 * 2 * mystery(1) = 5 * 4 * 3 * 2 * 1 = 120.

guiding_questions.text: What is the base case? When does the recursion stop?
Expand the call stack: mystery(5) = 5 * mystery(4) = … What is mystery(1)?

multiple_choice.source: AP CSA style — Unit 10 (string recursion, modeled after CSAwesome medium MCQ)
multiple_choice.prompt: Consider the following method. What is printed by  recur("hello") ?
multiple_choice.explanation: The recursive call happens BEFORE the print, so each character is printed on the way back up. The deepest call returns first, printing 'o', then 'l', 'l', 'e', 'h' → "olleh".

guiding_questions.text: Is the print statement before or after the recursive call? Why does that matter?
What string is passed in each recursive call? What is the last non-empty string before the base case?

multiple_choice.source: AP CSA style — Unit 10 (numeric recursion, modeled after Runestone Academy medium MCQ)
multiple_choice.prompt: Consider the following method. What is printed by  mystery(1234) ?
multiple_choice.explanation: Recursive calls proceed: mystery(1234) → mystery(123) → mystery(12) → mystery(1) → mystery(0) (base). Returning: prints 1, then 2, then 3, then 4 → "1234".

guiding_questions.text: Trace the recursive calls. What value of n triggers the base case (n <= 0)?
On the way back up the call stack, what does each frame print? Start from mystery(1).

multiple_choice.prompt: What is printed when the following code executes?
multiple_choice.explanation: == compares object references. a and b are two distinct String objects created with new, so == is false. .equals() compares content, which is the same, so it returns true.

guiding_questions.text: What does == compare when used with objects in Java — references or values?
What does .equals() compare for String objects?

multiple_choice.prompt: Which of the following correctly reverses the elements of an array  arr  of length  n  in place?

options.A: for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { int temp = arr[i]; arr[i] = arr[n-i]; arr[n-i] = temp; }
options.B: for (int i = 0; i < n / 2; i++) { int temp = arr[i]; arr[i] = arr[n-1-i]; arr[n-1-i] = temp; }
options.C: for (int i = 0; i < n / 2; i++) { arr[i] = arr[n-1-i]; }
options.D: for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { arr[i] = arr[n-1-i]; }
options.E: for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) { int temp = arr[i]; arr[i] = arr[n-i]; arr[n-i] = temp; }

multiple_choice.explanation: Option B correctly swaps arr[i] and arr[n-1-i] for the first half of the array only (i < n/2). Option A causes ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException when i=0 (arr[n] is out of bounds). Option C overwrites without saving the original.

guiding_questions.text: Why should the loop only run for the first half of the array (i < n/2)?
Why does option A cause an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException? What is arr[n] when the array has n elements?

multiple_choice.source: AP CSA style — Unit 8 (2022 Data FRQ context)
multiple_choice.prompt: A 2D array  data  has  data.length  rows and  data[0].length  columns. Which nested loop correctly visits every element exactly once, row by row?
multiple_choice.explanation: Option B uses correct 0-based indexing (r < data.length, c < data[0].length) and traverses row by row. A uses <= causing out-of-bounds. C skips row 0 and column 0. D traverses column by column. E uses data.length for columns, which only works on square matrices.

guiding_questions.text: Why should the loop condition be r < data.length rather than r <= data.length?
Is it always safe to use data[0].length for the column count? What if the rows have different lengths?

multiple_choice.prompt: Which of the following correctly declares and initializes a variable to store the value 3.14 in Java?
multiple_choice.explanation: 3.14 is a double literal in Java. Declaring pi as double and assigning 3.14 is correct. int cannot hold a decimal. Assigning a float in Java requires a suffix F (e.g., 3.14F). decimal is not a Java type.

guiding_questions.text: What is the default type of a decimal literal like 3.14 in Java — float or double?
What would happen if you tried to store 3.14 into an int variable?

multiple_choice.prompt: What is printed when the following code executes?
multiple_choice.explanation: The for-each loop multiplies: 1*2=2, 2*4=8, 8*6=48, 48*8=384.

guiding_questions.text: How does a for-each loop differ from a standard for loop? What variable gets assigned on each iteration?
Trace the product: product starts at 1. After processing 2 it becomes 2. What is it after 4?

options.D: Compile error: Shape variable cannot hold a ColoredCircle

multiple_choice.explanation: ColoredCircle does not override name(), so it inherits the name() from its immediate superclass Circle, which returns "Circle". Dynamic dispatch uses the runtime type's most specific override.

guiding_questions.text: ColoredCircle does not define name(). Which class does it inherit name() from?
In a three-level hierarchy (Shape → Circle → ColoredCircle), which override takes precedence?

frq_questions.exam_title: 2021 AP Computer Science A Free-Response Questions
frq_questions.title: WordMatch
frq_questions.context: This question involves the WordMatch class, which stores a secret string and provides methods that compare other strings to the secret string. The WordMatch class has a private String instance variable secret, a constructor (implementation not shown), and method stubs for the two methods you will write.

examples:
WordMatch game = new WordMatch("mississippi");
game.scoreGuess("i")    // returns 4    (4 occurrences × 1²)
game.scoreGuess("iss")  // returns 18   (2 occurrences × 3²)
game.scoreGuess("issipp") // returns 36  (1 occurrence × 6²)
game.scoreGuess("mississippi") // returns 121 (1 × 11²)

WordMatch game2 = new WordMatch("aaaabb");
game2.scoreGuess("a")    // returns 4   (4 × 1²)
game2.scoreGuess("aa")   // returns 12  (3 × 2²)
game2.scoreGuess("aaa")  // returns 18  (2 × 3²)
game2.scoreGuess("aabb") // returns 16  (1 × 4²)
game2.scoreGuess("c")    // returns 0   (0 × 1²)

parts.prompt:
Write the WordMatch method findBetterGuess, which returns the better guess of its two String parameters, guess1 and guess2.

If the scoreGuess method returns different values for guess1 and guess2, then the guess with the higher score is returned.

If the scoreGuess method returns the same value for guess1 and guess2, then the alphabetically greater guess is returned.

Assume that guess1 and guess2 are different strings.

Complete the findBetterGuess method.

/** Returns the better of two guesses, as described in part (b).
 *  Precondition: guess1 and guess2 are non-empty strings */
public String findBetterGuess(String guess1, String guess2)

frq_questions.exam_title: 2021 AP Computer Science A Free-Response Questions
frq_questions.title: CombinedTable

parts.prompt:
Write the complete CombinedTable class. A CombinedTable is composed of two SingleTable objects, table1 and table2.

Rules:
- A CombinedTable can seat two fewer customers than the total seats of its two SingleTable objects (two seats are lost when the tables are pushed together).
- If the two SingleTable objects are the same height, the desirability of the CombinedTable is the average of the two view quality ratings.
- If the two SingleTable objects are NOT the same height, the desirability is 10 units less than the average of the two view quality ratings.

The CombinedTable class must include:
- A constructor: public CombinedTable(SingleTable t1, SingleTable t2)
- A method: public int getNumSeats() — returns the number of seats
- A method: public double getDesirability() — returns the desirability

Note: Information about the individual tables must be obtained through the SingleTable methods; the CombinedTable class may not access any SingleTable instance variables directly.
examples: SingleTable t1 (4 seats, view=6, height A) + SingleTable t2 (2 seats, view=8, height A) → CombinedTable: 4 seats, desirability = (6+8)/2 = 7.0
SingleTable t3 (4 seats, view=10, height B) + SingleTable t4 (2 seats, view=2, height A) → CombinedTable: 4 seats, desirability = (10+2)/2 - 10 = -4.0

frq_questions.exam_title: 2021 AP Computer Science A Free-Response Questions
frq_questions.title: ClubMembers
frq_questions.context: The ClubMembers class maintains a list of current club members. A member is represented by the ClubMember class (implementation not shown). Key ClubMember methods: getStudentName() returns the String name, getGraduationYear() returns the int year, isInGoodStanding() returns a boolean.

parts.prompt:
Write the ClubMembers method addMembers. The method takes a String array names and an int gradYear. For each name in the names array (in order), it creates a new ClubMember with that name and gradYear and appends it to memberList. All newly added members should be in good standing.

Assume the ClubMember constructor has the signature: ClubMember(String name, int gradYear)

Complete the addMembers method.

public void addMembers(String[] names, int gradYear)

Write the ClubMembers method removeMembers. The method:
1. Returns an ArrayList containing all members who have graduated (graduation year <= year parameter) AND are in good standing.
2. Removes from memberList all members who have graduated (graduation year <= year), regardless of standing.
3. If no members meet the criteria for the return list, returns an empty ArrayList.

Complete the removeMembers method.

public ArrayList<ClubMember> removeMembers(int year)

frq_questions.exam_title: 2021 AP Computer Science A Free-Response Questions
frq_questions.title: ArrayResizer
frq_questions.context: This question involves manipulating a two-dimensional array of integers. You will write two static methods of the ArrayResizer class.

parts.prompt:
Write the static method isNonZeroRow, which returns true if and only if every value in row r of array2D is non-zero.

Precondition: r is a valid row index in array2D.
Postcondition: array2D is unchanged.

Complete the isNonZeroRow method.

public static boolean isNonZeroRow(int[][] array2D, int r)

Write the static method resize, which returns a new two-dimensional array containing only the rows from array2D in which all values are non-zero. The elements in the new array should appear in the same order as they appeared in the original array.

You must use numNonZeroRows and isNonZeroRow appropriately to receive full credit. Assume isNonZeroRow works as specified, regardless of what you wrote in part (a).

Complete the resize method.

public static int[][] resize(int[][] array2D)

frq_questions.exam_title: 2022 AP Computer Science A Free-Response Questions
frq_questions.title: Game
frq_questions.context:
This question involves the simulation of the play and scoring of a single-player video game. In this game a player attempts to complete three levels. Levels are numbered 1, 2, and 3. The player starts the game with a score of 0 and progresses through each level in order. The game ends when the player has completed all three levels or when the player has lost all three lives.

The Level class represents a level in the game (implementation not shown). Key method: public boolean goalReached() — returns true if the goal of the level has been reached.

The Game class is partially shown.

parts.prompt:
Write the Game method playLevel, which simulates one round of playing the given level.

Playing a level consists of making one or more plays. Each play has a random result:
- If the goal is reached (level.goalReached() returns true): the score increases by levelBonus[levelNum - 1] and the level is completed successfully.
- If the goal is not reached: the player loses a life (lives decreases by 1) and the round ends.

The level ends when either the goal is reached or the player loses a life.

Complete the playLevel method.

public void playLevel(int levelNum, Level level)

Write the Game method play, which simulates an entire game by playing each of the three levels in order.

The game begins with the player on level 1. The player advances to the next level after successfully completing a level. The game ends when the player completes all three levels or when lives reaches 0.

Assume playLevel works as specified regardless of what you wrote in part (a).

Complete the play method.

public void play(Level[] levels)

frq_questions.exam_title: 2022 AP Computer Science A Free-Response Questions
frq_questions.title: Textbook

parts.prompt:
Write the complete Textbook class, which is a subclass of Book.

A Textbook has an edition number (a positive integer) in addition to a title and price.

The Textbook class must:
1. Have a constructor: public Textbook(String title, double price, int edition)
2. Have a method: public int getEdition() — returns the edition number
3. Override getBookInfo() to return a String of the form: [Book's getBookInfo() result] + "-" + edition
4. Have a method: public boolean canSubstituteFor(Textbook other) — returns true if this Textbook can substitute for other. A Textbook is a valid substitute if it has the same title and its edition is greater than or equal to other's edition.

Information about the book title and price must be maintained in the Book class. Information about the edition must be maintained in the Textbook class.

Note: A Textbook object cannot directly access the private instance variables of Book.

frq_questions.exam_title: 2022 AP Computer Science A Free-Response Questions
frq_questions.title: ReviewAnalysis

parts.prompt:
Write the ReviewAnalysis method getAverageRating, which returns the average rating (arithmetic mean) of all elements of allReviews.

Precondition: allReviews contains at least one Review. No element of allReviews is null.

Complete the getAverageRating method.

public double getAverageRating()

Write the ReviewAnalysis method collectComments, which collects and formats comments that contain an exclamation point. The method returns an ArrayList<String> containing formatted versions of user comments from allReviews that contain at least one exclamation point.

Formatting rules for each qualifying comment:
- The returned String begins with the index of the Review in allReviews
- Immediately followed by a hyphen ("-")
- Followed by a copy of the original comment
- If the comment does not end with '.' or '!', a '.' is appended

If no qualifying comments exist, return an empty ArrayList.

Complete the collectComments method.

public ArrayList<String> collectComments()

frq_questions.exam_title: 2022 AP Computer Science A Free-Response Questions
frq_questions.title: Data
frq_questions.context: This question involves the Data class, which stores a 2D grid of integer values and provides methods to analyze and repopulate the grid.

parts.prompt:
Write the Data method repopulate, which assigns each element of grid a randomly generated value according to the following rules:
- Each value is between 1 and MAX inclusive
- Each value is divisible by 10
- Each value is NOT divisible by 100

All such values have the same probability of being chosen. Recall that MAX = 100.

Complete the repopulate method.

public void repopulate()
parts.notes: Valid values between 1 and 100 divisible by 10 but not by 100 are: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, 70, 80, 90 (9 values).

parts.prompt:
Write the Data method findColumn, which returns the index of the column of grid that has the largest number of values greater than the column average. If there is a tie, return the smaller index.

Assume repopulate works as specified regardless of what you wrote in part (a).

Complete the findColumn method.

public int findColumn()

frq_questions.exam_title: 2023 AP Computer Science A Free-Response Questions
frq_questions.title: AppointmentBook
frq_questions.context:
This question involves the AppointmentBook class, which provides methods for students to schedule appointments with their teacher. Appointments can be scheduled during one of eight class periods during the school day, numbered 1 through 8. Each period has 60 minutes, numbered 0 through 59.

The AppointmentBook class uses a boolean 2D array minutesReserved where minutesReserved[p][m] indicates whether minute m in period p is already reserved. Periods are indexed 1–8 (index 0 unused).

parts.prompt:
Write the AppointmentBook method findFreeBlock. The method searches period for the first block of consecutive free minutes that is duration minutes long. If such a block is found, findFreeBlock returns the first minute in the block. Otherwise, findFreeBlock returns -1.

A minute is free if minutesReserved[period][minute] is false.

Precondition: 1 <= period <= 8, 1 <= duration <= 60.

Complete the findFreeBlock method.

public int findFreeBlock(int period, int duration)
examples: // If period 2 is entirely free, findFreeBlock(2, 15) returns 0
// If minutes 0–19 in period 3 are reserved, findFreeBlock(3, 30) returns 20

parts.prompt:
Write the AppointmentBook method makeAppointment. The method searches the periods from startPeriod to endPeriod, inclusive, for the earliest free block of duration consecutive available minutes in the lowest-numbered period possible.

If such a block is found, makeAppointment calls reserveBlock to mark those minutes as reserved and returns true. If no such block is found in any of the periods, makeAppointment returns false.

Assume findFreeBlock works as specified regardless of what you wrote in part (a).

Precondition: 1 <= startPeriod <= endPeriod <= 8, 1 <= duration <= 60.

Complete the makeAppointment method.

public boolean makeAppointment(int startPeriod, int endPeriod, int duration)

frq_questions.exam_title: 2023 AP Computer Science A Free-Response Questions
frq_questions.title: Sign
frq_questions.context: This question involves methods that distribute text across the lines of an electronic sign. The electronic sign and the text to be displayed are represented by the Sign class.

parts.prompt:
Write the complete Sign class. The Sign class stores a message String and an int lineWidth representing how many characters fit on one line.

The Sign class must include:
1. A constructor: public Sign(String message, int lineWidth)
2. A method: public int numberOfLines() — returns the number of full-width lines needed to display the message. A line is full if it contains exactly lineWidth characters. If the message has remaining characters that don't fill a full line, they are NOT counted as an additional line. If the message is empty, returns 0.
3. A method: public String getLines() — returns a String containing the message broken into segments of lineWidth characters, separated by semicolons. If the message is the empty string, returns null. No semicolon appears after the last segment.

Note: The message may be any non-null String.

parts.prompt:

public void cleanData(double lower, double upper)

The boundary parameter is a double array where boundary[k] is the boundary temperature for day k.

The method returns the length of the longest heat wave found. If no heat wave exists, return 0.

Assume cleanData works as specified regardless of what you wrote in part (a).

frq_questions.title: BoxOfCandy
frq_questions.context: The BoxOfCandy class represents a candy box where the candy is arranged in a rectangular grid. The instance variable box is a 2D array of Candy objects. A null entry means that position is empty. The Candy class has a getFlavor() method that returns a String.

parts.prompt:
Write the BoxOfCandy method moveCandyToFirstRow. The method ensures that the position box[0][col] contains a piece of candy.

If box[0][col] already has candy, the method does nothing and returns true.
If box[0][col] is null, the method searches the rest of column col (rows 1 through box.length - 1) for a candy. If found, it moves that candy to box[0][col], sets the original position to null, and returns true.
If no candy exists anywhere in column col, the method returns false.

Precondition: col is a valid column index in box.

Complete the moveCandyToFirstRow method.

public boolean moveCandyToFirstRow(int col)

Write the BoxOfCandy method removeNextByFlavor. The method removes and returns the first candy with the given flavor found by traversing the box in the following order: the LAST row is traversed from left to right, then the next-to-last row from left to right, and so on up to row 0.

If a matching candy is found, it is replaced with null in the box and returned.
If no matching candy is found, the method returns null.

Assume moveCandyToFirstRow works as specified regardless of what you wrote in part (a).

Complete the removeNextByFlavor method.

frq_questions.context: This question involves a bird feeder simulation. On any given day, only birds visit the feeder. Each bird consumes between 10 and 50 grams of food, inclusive, with each integer amount equally likely. The simulation tracks the amount of food available and simulates multiple days.

parts.prompt:
Write the Feeder method simulateManyDays, which simulates multiple days of feeding and returns the number of days simulated.

The simulation continues until foodAvailable drops below minFood. The method calls simulateOneDay each day (passing numBirds) and counts the days. When foodAvailable < minFood after a day's simulation, the simulation ends and the total number of days is returned.

Assume simulateOneDay works as specified regardless of what you wrote in part (a).

frq_questions.exam_title: 2024 AP Computer Science A Free-Response Questions
frq_questions.title: Scoreboard
frq_questions.context: This question involves a scoreboard for a two-team game. The game is played with two teams who alternate turns. At any given time, one team is active (it is their turn) and the other team is inactive. The game always begins with team 1 as the active team.

parts.prompt:
Write the complete Scoreboard class.

The game is played between two teams who alternate turns, so at any given time, one team is active and the other is inactive.

The Scoreboard class must include:
1. A constructor: public Scoreboard(String team1, String team2)
   - team1 is the name of team 1, team2 is the name of team 2
   - The game always begins with team 1 as the active team and both scores at 0
2. A method: public void recordPlay(int points)
   - If points > 0: the active team's score increases by points and the active team remains active
   - If points == 0: the active team's turn ends; the inactive team becomes active
   - points is guaranteed to be nonnegative
3. A method: public String getScore()
   - Returns a String formatted as: score1 + "-" + score2 + "-" + activeTeamName
   - Where score1 is team 1's score, score2 is team 2's score, and activeTeamName is the name of the currently active team
examples:
Scoreboard sb = new Scoreboard("Tigers", "Lions");
sb.getScore()          // returns "0-0-Tigers" (team 1 starts active)
sb.recordPlay(7);      // Tigers score 7; still Tigers' turn
sb.getScore()          // returns "7-0-Tigers"
sb.recordPlay(0);      // Tigers' turn ends; Lions become active
sb.getScore()          // returns "7-0-Lions"
sb.recordPlay(3);      // Lions score 3; still Lions' turn
sb.getScore()          // returns "7-3-Lions"

frq_questions.exam_title: 2024 AP Computer Science A Free-Response Questions
frq_questions.title: WordChecker
frq_questions.context: This question involves the WordChecker class, which analyzes a list of words stored in an ArrayList<String>. You will write two methods of the WordChecker class.

parts.prompt:
Write the WordChecker method isWordChain. A word list is a word chain if each word in the list (except the first) contains the previous word as a substring.

In other words: for each index i from 1 to wordList.size() - 1, wordList.get(i) contains wordList.get(i-1) as a substring.

Return true if the list is a word chain (or if the list has 0 or 1 elements), false otherwise.

Complete the isWordChain method.

public boolean isWordChain()
examples:
// ["cat", "locate", "dislocate"] → true  ("locate" contains "cat"; "dislocate" contains "locate")
// ["cat", "catalog", "disc"] → false  ("disc" does not contain "catalog")

parts.prompt:
Write the WordChecker method createList. The method creates and returns a new ArrayList<String> containing only the words from wordList that begin with the String target. For each such word, the target is removed from the beginning before adding it to the new list.

The order of words in the new list should match their original order in wordList.

If no words in wordList begin with target, return an empty ArrayList.

Assume isWordChain works as specified regardless of what you wrote in part (a).

Complete the createList method.

public ArrayList<String> createList(String target)

frq_questions.title: GridPath
frq_questions.context: This question involves a 2D integer grid and methods to traverse a path through it. You will write two methods of the GridPath class. A path through the grid starts at a given location and moves to neighboring cells based on their values.

parts.prompt:
Write the GridPath method getNextLoc. From position (row, col), the method considers two neighbors:
- The cell directly below: (row + 1, col)
- The cell directly to the right: (row, col + 1)

The method returns the Location of the neighbor with the smaller value. If both neighbors have the same value, return the location below. If only one neighbor exists (at an edge), return that neighbor's location.

Precondition: (row, col) is not in the last row AND not in the last column — i.e., at least one valid neighbor always exists.

Complete the getNextLoc method.

public Location getNextLoc(int row, int col)

Write the GridPath method sumPath. Starting at (startRow, startCol), the path continues by repeatedly calling getNextLoc until the current location is in both the last row and the last column (bottom-right corner). The method returns the sum of all values along the path, including the starting cell and the ending cell.

Assume getNextLoc works as specified regardless of what you wrote in part (a).

Complete the sumPath method.

public int sumPath(int startRow, int startCol)
